---
title: "assemble_databse"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-11"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library("withr", lib.loc= "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(dplyr , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(tidyr , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(ggplot2 , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(viridis , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(stringr , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(patchwork , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(data.table , lib.loc = "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(foreach, lib.loc= "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library(ggseqlogo , lib.loc= "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")

library("labeling", lib.loc= "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")
library("isoband", lib.loc= "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")

library("withr", lib.loc= "/doctorai/niccoloc/libR2")

```



```{r load raw db}


path_db_chiara = '/doctorai/chiarba/dataset/FINAL/MS_BCR_DB_Final_Version_seqID_UPDATED.tsv'
path_db_chiara = '/doctorai/chiarba/dataset/FINAL/MS_BCR_DB_Final_Version_seqID.tsv'
db_raw=fread(path_db_chiara) 
View(db_raw)
head(db_raw)


```


```{r fucntions }

# ----------------------------
# 1) Helper functions
# ----------------------------
merge_columns_safe <- function(df,
                               col_from,
                               col_to,
                               drop_source = FALSE,
                               stop_on_conflict = TRUE,
                               verbose = TRUE) {
  
  # ---- Check columns exist ----
  if (!col_from %in% colnames(df)) {
    stop(paste("Column", col_from, "not found in dataframe"))
  }
  if (!col_to %in% colnames(df)) {
    stop(paste("Column", col_to, "not found in dataframe"))
  }
  
  # ---- Extract vectors ----
  from_vec <- df[[col_from]]
  to_vec   <- df[[col_to]]
  
  # ---- Define what "missing" means ----
  is_missing <- function(x) {
    is.na(x) | trimws(as.character(x)) == ""
  }
  
  from_missing <- is_missing(from_vec)
  to_missing   <- is_missing(to_vec)
  
  from_not_missing <- !from_missing
  to_not_missing   <- !to_missing
  
  # ---- Conflict definition ----
  # Conflict ONLY if both are non-missing AND different
  conflict_mask <- from_not_missing & 
                   to_not_missing & 
                   (trimws(as.character(from_vec)) != trimws(as.character(to_vec)))
  
  n_conflicts <- sum(conflict_mask)
  
  # ---- Reporting ----
  if (verbose) {
    cat("---- Merge Report ----\n")
    cat("Rows only in", col_from, ":", sum(from_not_missing & to_missing), "\n")
    cat("Rows only in", col_to, ":", sum(from_missing & to_not_missing), "\n")
    cat("Rows both identical :", sum(from_not_missing & to_not_missing & !conflict_mask), "\n")
    cat("Rows conflicting     :", n_conflicts, "\n")
  }
  
  if (n_conflicts > 0 && stop_on_conflict) {
    stop("Conflicting non-missing values detected. Merge aborted.")
  } else if (n_conflicts > 0) {
    warning("Conflicting non-missing values detected. Merge will proceed but check results.")
    #concated the conflicted labels with a separator (e.g. " ") to keep the information from both columns
    df[[col_to]][conflict_mask] <- paste0(df[[col_to]][conflict_mask], " ", from_vec[conflict_mask])
    
    
  }
  
  # ---- Update col_to where it is missing and not conflicting ---
  update_mask <- from_not_missing & to_missing & !conflict_mask
  df[[col_to]][update_mask] <- from_vec[update_mask]
  
  # ---- Optionally drop source column ----
  if (drop_source) {
    df[[col_from]] <- NULL
  }
  
  return(df)
}




# Strip allele from AIRR calls, e.g. "IGHV1-69*01" -> "IGHV1-69"
strip_allele <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x)) return(x)
  x <- as.character(x)
  # Keep only before '*' if present
  sub("\\*.*$", "", x)
}

# Infer receptor locus (IGH/IGK/IGL) from v_call or v_gene
infer_locus_from_v <- function(v) {
  v <- as.character(v)
  locus <- case_when(
    str_detect(v, "^IGH") ~ "IGH",
    str_detect(v, "^IGK") ~ "IGK",
    str_detect(v, "^IGL") ~ "IGL",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
  locus
}
```


```{r database preparation}
db_ms=db_raw #%>% filter( disease_diagnosis !="Healthy")

#look for missing columns and sparse metadata ~ handled separately in the annotations table
missing_summary <- db_ms %>%
  select(-ReleaseDate, -create_date, -version) %>% 
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.) | . =="" ))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "missing_fraction") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_fraction))

View(missing_summary)

#merge columns with same annotations 
db_ms <- merge_columns_safe(db_ms,
                         col_from = "cell_type",
                         col_to   = "cell_subtype",
                         drop_source = TRUE, stop_on_conflict = FALSE)
#check the conficting rows
conflicts <- db_ms %>%
  filter(is.na(cell_type) == FALSE & is.na(cell_subtype) == FALSE & trimws(as.character(cell_type)) != trimws(as.character(cell_subtype))) %>%
  select(sequence_id, cell_type, cell_subtype)

#unifrom the cell types annotations

db_ms <- db_ms %>%
  mutate(
    cell_subtype = str_trim(cell_subtype),
    cell_subtype = case_when(
      
      # Fix spelling
      cell_subtype == "witched memory B cell" ~ "class-switched memory B cell",
      
      # Standardize lymphocyte -> B cell
      cell_subtype %in% c("Memory B lymphocyte") ~ "Memory B cell",
      cell_subtype %in% c("Naive B lymphocyte") ~ "Naive B cell",
      cell_subtype %in% c("Double Negative B lymphocyte") ~ "double negative B cell",
      
      # Merge plasmablast labels
      cell_subtype %in% c("Plasmablast",
                          "plasmablast/plasma cell B cell") ~ "Plasmablast",
      
      # Capitalization normalization
      cell_subtype == "Naive B cell" ~ "Naive B cell",
      cell_subtype == "double negative B cell" ~ "double negative B cell",
      cell_subtype == "regulatory B cell" ~ "regulatory B cell",
      cell_subtype == "transitional Breg" ~ "transitional Breg",
      cell_subtype == "B cell" ~ "B cell",
      cell_subtype == "total B cells" ~ "total B cells",
      
      # Empty -> NA
      cell_subtype == "" ~ NA_character_,
      
      TRUE ~ cell_subtype
    )
  )

#updated view of missing after update
missing_summary2 <- db_ms %>%
  select(-ReleaseDate, -create_date, -version) %>% 
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.) | . =="" ))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "missing_fraction") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_fraction))
View(missing_summary2)


main_cols=missing_summary %>% 
  filter(missing_fraction < 0.5) %>% pull(column)

db_main = db_ms %>% select(all_of(main_cols))  

#check for duplicated ids
db_main %>%
  group_by(sequence_id) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  arrange(sequence_id)

 
#correct the naming for lomakin zyg study
db_main$subject_number %>% unique() %>% sort()
# Z5 and Z3 become Lo5 and lo3
db_main$subject_number <- case_when(
  db_main$subject_number == "Z5" ~ "Lo5",
  db_main$subject_number == "Z3" ~ "Lo3",
  TRUE ~ db_main$subject_number
)



colnames(db_main)
# ----------------------------
# 2) Build gene-level calls (no allele)
# ----------------------------

db_main <- db_main %>%
  mutate(
    v_gene = strip_allele(v_call),
    d_gene = strip_allele(d_call),
    j_gene = strip_allele(j_call)
  )

# ----------------------------
# 3) junction_length_aa
# ----------------------------

db_main= db_main %>%
  mutate(
    junction_length_aa = str_length(junction_aa)
  )
# ----------------------------
# 4) Receptor type: heavy / kappa / lambda
# ----------------------------
db_main <- db_main %>%
    mutate(
      locus = case_when(
        str_detect(toupper(v_call), "IGH|HEAVY") ~ "IGH",
        str_detect(toupper(v_call), "IGK|KAPPA") ~ "IGK",
        str_detect(toupper(v_call), "IGL|LAMBDA") ~ "IGL",
        TRUE ~ NA
      )
 )
table(db_main$locus , db_main$study)
table(db_main$study , db_main$locus)
table(db_main$study , db_main$rev_comp)
 
# ----------------------------
# 5) Build CORE features
# ----------------------------
# Core must contain identity + biology always

core_cols <- c(
  "sequence_id",
  "clone_id",
  "study",
  "subject_number",
  "junction_aa",
  "v_gene",
  "d_gene",
  "j_gene",
  "junction_length_aa",
  "productive",
  "disease_diagnosis",
  "tissue",
  "tissue_type",
  "study",'sex', "locus", "cell_subtype"
)
setdiff( colnames(db_main), core_cols)

missing_core <- setdiff(core_cols, colnames(db_main))
if (length(missing_core) > 0) {
  warning(paste0("Some core columns are missing and will be skipped: ", paste(missing_core, collapse = ", ")))
}




core_df <- db_main %>% filter( disease_diagnosis =="Healthy")%>% select(any_of(core_cols))

#lightweight version of the datbase with only the core features, to be used for general usage, the annotations and miscellaneouse data are separated and easily mergeable for users interested in specific analyses
core_df <- core_df %>%
  mutate(
    study = as.factor(study),
    subject_number = as.factor(subject_number),
    v_gene = as.factor(v_gene),
    d_gene = as.factor(d_gene),
    j_gene = as.factor(j_gene)
  )

fwrite(core_df, "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/MS_BCR/MS_BCR_db_core.csv")



#alignment and full sequence dataset


alignment_columns <- c(
  "sequence_id",  "duplicate_count",  "sequence",  "sequence_alignment",
  "germline_alignment",  "v_score",  "d_score",  "j_score",  "v_cigar",
  "d_cigar",  "j_cigar",  "v_alignment_start",  "v_alignment_end",  "d_alignment_start",  "d_alignment_end",  "j_alignment_start",
  "j_alignment_end",  "v_germline_start",  "v_germline_end",  "j_germline_start",
  "j_germline_end",  "v_sequence_start",  "v_sequence_end",
  "d_call",  "j_sequence_start",  "j_sequence_end",  "np2",  "d_sequence_start",  "d_sequence_end",
  "SRA_id"    ,  "d_cigar",  "np2_length",  "d_germline_start",
  "d_sequence_start",  "d_germline_end",  "d_sequence_end",  "d_alignment_start"  ,
  "d_alignment_end",  "sequence_id_old",  "np1",  "c_call",  "cdr3",  "cdr3_aa",  "j_alignment_start",
  "j_alignment_end",  "v_alignment_start",  "v_alignment_end" ,
  "sequence",  "rev_comp",  "productive",   "v_call",   "j_call",   "sequence_alignment",
  "germline_alignment" ,   "complete_vdj",  "junction" ,   "v_score",   "v_cigar",   "j_score",  "j_cigar",  "junction_length"  ,
  "np1_length",  "v_germline_start" ,  "v_sequence_start" ,  "v_germline_end",   "v_sequence_end",
  "j_germline_start",   "j_sequence_start","fwr2", "fwr2_aa","fwr1","fwr1_aa","cdr1","cdr1_aa","cdr2","cdr2_aa","fwr3","fwr3_aa","fwr4","fwr4_aa",
  "j_germline_end",   "j_sequence_end"
)

db_alignment <- db_ms %>% select(any_of(alignment_columns), 'sequence_id')
fwrite(db_alignment, "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/MS_BCR/MS_BCR_db_alignment.csv")


#summary to check which column will be part of core, alignment, misceallenous
missing_summary3 <- db_main %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.) | . =="" ))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "missing_fraction") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_fraction))
View(missing_summary3)

db_columns=missing_summary3 %>% 
  mutate(db_type = case_when(
    column %in% core_cols ~ 'core',
    column %in% alignment_columns ~  'align',
    TRUE ~ 'other'))   # %>% fwrite('/doctorai/niccoloc/cols_ab.csv')
View(db_columns)

other_cols=db_columns %>%  filter(db_type=='other') %>% pull(column)

db_others= db_ms %>% select(any_of(other_cols), 'sequence_id')
fwrite(db_others, "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/MS_BCR/MS_BCR_db_miscellaneous.csv")


# unique_summary <- db_ms %>%
#   summarise(across(everything(), ~ n_distinct(.))) %>%
#   pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
#                names_to = "column",
#                values_to = "n_unique") %>%
#   arrange(n_unique)
# 
# unique_summary

```
 
```{r study-specific metadata}



# ============================================
# Sparse metadata -> LONG "annotations" table

# ============================================

library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(janitor)

# ----------------------------
# Helper: treat NA and "" as missing
# ----------------------------
is_missing <- function(x) {
  is.na(x) | trimws(as.character(x)) == ""
}

# ----------------------------
# 1) Build annotations table (LONG)
# ----------------------------
# df: original dataframe
# id_cols: keys to keep (must include sequence_id)
# threshold: missing fraction threshold to define "sparse" - 0.5 means keep columns with >=50% missing
# keep_also: columns you always want to keep in core (excluded from sparse selection)
# out_path: where to save annotations
build_sparse_annotations <- function(df,
                                    id_cols = c("sequence_id"),
                                    context_cols = c("study", "subject_number"),
                                    threshold = 0.5,
                                    keep_also = character(0),
                                    out_path = "annotations_sparse_long.csv",
                                    write_to_disk = FALSE,
                                    verbose = TRUE) {
  # Clean names once (safe even if already clean)
  df[df == "NA"] <- NA
  df[df == ""]  <- NA
  
  # Checks
  missing_ids <- setdiff(id_cols, colnames(df))
  if (length(missing_ids) > 0) {
    stop(paste0("Missing id columns: ", paste(missing_ids, collapse = ", ")))
  }
  
  # Missing fraction per column (NA + "" considered missing)
  miss_frac <- sapply(df, function(col) mean(is_missing(col)))
  
  # Choose sparse columns
  exclude_cols <- unique(c(id_cols, context_cols, keep_also))
  sparse_cols <- names(miss_frac)[miss_frac >= threshold]
  sparse_cols <- setdiff(sparse_cols, exclude_cols)
  #add to sparse columns "time_point"  and   "treatment"
  sparse_cols <- unique(c(sparse_cols))
  
  if (verbose) {
    cat("Sparse columns selected:", length(sparse_cols), "\n")
    if (length(sparse_cols) > 0) {
      print(sort(miss_frac[sparse_cols], decreasing = TRUE)[1:min(25, length(sparse_cols))])
    }
  }
  
  if (length(sparse_cols) == 0) {
    warning("No sparse columns found for given threshold.")
    return(list(annotations = NULL, sparse_cols = character(0), missing_fraction = miss_frac))
  }
  
  # Keep only context columns that exist
  context_cols <- intersect(context_cols, colnames(df))
  
  #convert all the sparse columns to character (to avoid issues with factors, numerics, etc.)
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(sparse_cols), as.character))
  
  # Build LONG annotations: one row per (sequence_id, field, value)
  annotations <- df %>%
    select(any_of(c(id_cols, context_cols, sparse_cols))) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(sparse_cols),
      names_to = "field",
      values_to = "value"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      value = trimws(as.character(value)),
      field = as.character(field)
    ) %>%
    filter(!(is.na(value) | value == "")) %>%   # keep only real values
    distinct()
  
  if (write_to_disk) {
  fwrite(annotations, out_path)
  }
  
  if (verbose) {
    cat("Saved annotations to:", out_path, "\n")
    cat("Rows in annotations:", nrow(annotations), "\n")
  }
  
  return(list(
    annotations = annotations,
    sparse_cols = sparse_cols,
    missing_fraction = miss_frac
  ))
}

# ----------------------------
# 2) Merge-back utilities (for users)
# ----------------------------



# A) Convert LONG annotations back to WIDE (one col per field)
annotations_long_to_wide <- function(annotations,
                                    id_cols = c("sequence_id"),
                                    context_cols = c("study", "subject_number")) {
  # Keep only present columns
  context_cols <- intersect(context_cols, colnames(annotations))
  id_cols <- intersect(id_cols, colnames(annotations))
  
  annotations %>%
    select(any_of(c(id_cols, context_cols, "field", "value"))) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = field,
      values_from = value
    )
}

# B) Merge WIDE annotations into a core table (left join by keys)
merge_annotations_into_core <- function(db_ms,
                                       annotations_long,
                                       by_cols = c("sequence_id")) {
  ann_wide <- annotations_long_to_wide(
    annotations_long,
    id_cols = by_cols,
    context_cols = setdiff(intersect(c("study", "subject_number"), colnames(annotations_long)), by_cols)
  )
  
  core_df %>%
    left_join(ann_wide, by = by_cols)
}

# ----------------------------
# Example pipeline
# ----------------------------
removed_cols =c( "fwr2", "fwr2_aa","fwr1","fwr1_aa","cdr1","cdr1_aa","cdr2","cdr2_aa","fwr3","fwr3_aa","fwr4","fwr4_aa")
# Load original DB sparse columns
study_spec_cols=missing_summary2 %>% 
  filter(missing_fraction > 0.5) %>% pull(column)

 


# 1) Build annotations for columns with missing fraction >= 0.5  
res <- build_sparse_annotations(
  df = db_ms %>% select(all_of(study_spec_cols), "sequence_id") %>% 
    select(-any_of(removed_cols)),
  id_cols = c("sequence_id"),
  context_cols = c("study", "subject_number"),
  write_to_disk = T,
  threshold = 0.5,
  # keep_also = c("clone_id", "junction_aa", "v_gene", "d_gene", "j_gene"), # optional
  out_path = "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/MS_BCR/MS_BCR_db_metadata_sparse_long.csv"
)


# 2) Later, a user can merge back:
core_df <- fread("MS_BCR_db_core.csv", data.table = FALSE)
annotations <- fread( "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/MS_BCR/metadata_sparse_long.csv", data.table = FALSE)

core_plus <- merge_annotations_into_core(core_df, annotations, by_cols = c("sequence_id"))




```

```{r tabel for meatadata sparsity x study}
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)

# Helper: missing definition
is_missing <- function(x) {
  is.na(x) | trimws(as.character(x)) == ""
}

build_study_sparse_coverage <- function(df,
                                        study_col = "study",
                                        sparse_cols,
                                        output_path = NULL,
                                        verbose = TRUE) {
  
  # Check study column
  if (!study_col %in% colnames(df)) {
    stop(paste("Study column", study_col, "not found"))
  }
  
  # Keep only existing sparse columns
  sparse_cols <- intersect(sparse_cols, colnames(df))
  
  if (length(sparse_cols) == 0) {
    stop("No valid sparse columns found in dataframe")
  }
  
  # Convert sparse cols to character (safe)
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(sparse_cols), as.character))
  
  # Compute % non-missing per study per column
  coverage_long <- df %>%
    group_by(.data[[study_col]]) %>%
    summarise(across(
      all_of(sparse_cols),
      ~ mean(!is_missing(.)) * 100,
      .names = "{.col}"
    ),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -all_of(study_col),
      names_to = "field",
      values_to = "percent_annotated"
    )
  
  # Convert to wide matrix
  coverage_wide <- coverage_long %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = field,
      values_from = percent_annotated
    ) %>%
    arrange(.data[[study_col]])
  
  if (!is.null(output_path)) {
    fwrite(coverage_wide, output_path)
  }
  
  if (verbose) {
    cat("Number of studies:", n_distinct(df[[study_col]]), "\n")
    cat("Sparse metadata fields analyzed:", length(sparse_cols), "\n")
  }
  
  return(coverage_wide)
}


coverage_table <- build_study_sparse_coverage(
  df = db_ms,
  study_col = "study",
  sparse_cols = study_spec_cols,
  output_path = "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/study_sparse_metadata_coverage.csv"
)

View(coverage_table)
fwrite(coverage_table, "/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/study_sparse_metadata_coverage.csv")


```


```{r get only the healthy for the core database}

ms_hc=fread("/doctorai/chiarba/analysis/AT_THE_END/MS_HC_2026_h0h1.tsv") %>% filter(disease_diagnosis=="Healthy")



missing_hc = ms_hc %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(is.na(.) | . =="" ))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "column",
               values_to = "missing_fraction") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_fraction))

hc_cols=missing_hc %>%filter(missing_fraction < 0.5) %>% pull(column)

ms_hc %>% 
  select(any_of(hc_cols)) %>% fwrite("/doctorai/niccoloc/MS_db/MS_BCR_healthy_ONLY.csv")





```
